## Sub-workflow- Extract Bill of Lading Data
**Double click** to edit me. [Guide](https://docs.n8n.io/workflows/components/sticky-notes/)

## Sub-workflow- Extract Packing List Data
**Double click** to edit me. [Guide](https://docs.n8n.io/workflows/components/sticky-notes/)

parameters.text:
You are a logistics data extractor. Output ONLY the JSON object below. No markdown. No explanation.

OUTPUT SCHEMA (fill every field — use 0 / "" / [] for missing values, never null):
{
  "factory":            { "name": "", "address": "" },
  "consignee":          { "name": "", "address": "" },
  "incoterm_location":  "",
  "logistics_metrics":  { "pallet_count": 0, "carton_count": 0, "piece_count": 0, "gross_weight_kg": 0.0, "volume_cbm": 0.0 },
  "containers":         [ { "container_number": "", "seal_number": "", "type": "", "package_count": 0, "gross_weight_kg": 0.0, "volume_cbm": 0.0 } ],
  "cargo_description":  "",
  "document_type":      "HBL"
}

FIELD RULES:
factory.name/address    → SHIPPER field
consignee.name/address  → CONSIGNEE field
incoterm_location       → PORT OF LOADING — city name only (e.g. "QINGDAO")
gross_weight_kg         → GROSS WEIGHT (KGS) — number only, no units
volume_cbm              → MEASUREMENT (CBM) — number only, no units
cargo_description       → DESCRIPTION OF GOODS — comma-separated if multiple types

PACKAGING — two formats, detect which applies:

FORMAT A — single container (no container table on the document):
  Read "TOTAL NUMBER OF PACKAGES (IN WORDS)" sentence, e.g. "SAY TWELVE PALLETS ONLY"
  Unit = PALLET/PALLETS/PLT  → pallet_count = that number, carton_count = 0
  Unit = CARTON/CARTONS/CTN  → carton_count = that number, pallet_count = 0
  piece_count = 0 unless stated separately
  containers = []

FORMAT B — multiple containers (a container table is present):
  IGNORE "TOTAL NUMBER OF PACKAGES (IN WORDS)" for pallet/carton counts
  READ the cargo description block for a breakdown e.g. "140PALLETS+300PIECES=440PACKAGES"
  pallet_count → PALLETS/PLT figure in that expression
  piece_count  → PIECES/PCS figure (0 if absent)
  carton_count → CARTONS/CTN figure (0 if absent)
  containers   → one object per table row: container_number, seal_number, type, package_count, gross_weight_kg, volume_cbm

Output ONLY the JSON. No markdown. No explanation.

parameters.content:
## Sub-workflow- Convert XLS to PDF
**Double click** to edit me. [Guide](https://docs.n8n.io/workflows/components/sticky-notes/)

parameters.text:
You are a logistics data extractor. Output ONLY the JSON object below. No markdown. No explanation.
This document is from one of 200+ suppliers — field names vary widely. Use the lookup tables below.
Always read GRAND TOTAL / TOTAL rows. Never use individual line items.

OUTPUT SCHEMA (fill every field — use 0 / "" / [] for missing values, never null):
{
  "factory":            { "name": "", "address": "" },
  "consignee":          { "name": "", "address": "" },
  "incoterm_location":  "",
  "logistics_metrics":  { "pallet_count": 0, "carton_count": 0, "piece_count": 0, "gross_weight_kg": 0.0, "volume_cbm": 0.0 },
  "containers":         [ { "container_number": "", "package_count": 0, "gross_weight_kg": 0.0, "volume_cbm": 0.0 } ],
  "cargo_description":  "",
  "document_type":      "Packing List"
}

FIELD LOOKUP TABLE:
factory.name/address   → header sender: Shipper / From / Supplier / Manufacturer / Seller / company name at top
consignee.name/address → recipient: TO: / Ship To: / Consignee / Buyer / Customer / Sold To
incoterm_location      → TERMS OF DELIVERY / Incoterm / Delivery Terms / Shipment Terms / FOB Point — city name only
                         If value is a trade term only (FOB / CIF / EXW etc) with no city → return ""
gross_weight_kg        → G.W.(Kgs) / Gross Weight / Total G.W. / GW / Total Weight / Gross Wt. — number only
volume_cbm             → Total CBM / Measurement / CBM / M3 / Volume / Total M3 / Meas. — number only
cargo_description      → Item Name / Description / Product / Goods / Article / Commodity — comma-separated if multiple

PACKAGING LOOKUP TABLE (from grand total row only):
pallet_count → PLTS / PLT NO. / Pallets / No. of Pallets / Pallet Qty / Total Pallets / NB PALLETS / PALLET
carton_count → TOTAL CARTON / CTNS / CTN / Cartons / Package (CTNS) / No. of Cartons / Boxes / Total Boxes
piece_count  → PCS / Pieces / Units / Total Qty / Total PCS / QTY (PCS)
               ONLY for individual loose items — NOT for carton or pallet counts. If ambiguous → 0
If total row has both pallets and cartons → set both. Missing type → 0.

CONTAINERS:
A container number is a standardized shipping identifier: 4 letters + 7 digits (e.g. EGHU3672790, BEAU4662872).
ONLY extract containers array if the document has explicit sections headed by a real container number.
Container section headings: "Container No.:" / "CNTR:" / "Container#" / "CONTAINER NO" / "CTNR #"
  Extract the SUBTOTAL row of each section → one object per container
  package_count → PALLETS figure from that subtotal row, NOT cartons
  Only use carton count if no pallet figure exists in that subtotal row
DO NOT treat pallet numbers (NO.1, NO.2, PLT 1 etc) as container numbers.
DO NOT treat row numbers or sequence numbers as container numbers.
If no valid container sections exist → containers = []
